steps.title: Identify the moisture-to-fat relationship
steps.content: The passage establishes that water evaporates from the crust, creating voids that oil fills, thereby increasing fat content. This creates a direct link: greater moisture loss leads to greater fat gain.

steps.title: Apply the inverse relationship
steps.content:
If less moisture is lost, fewer voids form and less fat is gained—exactly what choice A states.

The other three introduce unsupported factors (B's temperature), misapply the mechanism (C's permeability), or reverse the expected outcome (D's crispiness).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
